notes: Also genitive suffix -lu; omitted in company/agency names
Oldest verb; culturally significant; predates infinitive system
Root nøvd-; used in formal request phrase

examples.en: I formally request the account, please.

examples.sh: Da søvæ a *sha* ən gə dənsh.
examples.en: I see a *shark* in the ocean.
My apologies, I did not know.

notes: Used in very formal apology phrase

examples.sh: Køpæ a sha vel a døgí.
examples.en: Buy a shark or a dog.

examples.sh: Da wølæ ve, mæk da ni hævæ køpøn.
examples.en: I want it, but I have no money.

examples.sh: Da gønæ dæn da nødæ køng.
examples.en: I go because I need water.

examples.sh: Gə sha de øv gə ji.
examples.en: The shark is on the sand.

notes: str- cluster; distinct from legislative house

examples.sh: Gə pørsønew gønæ æt gə strøvøksi.
examples.en: The people go to the city.
I am a citizen of Shæwaka.

notes: Compound: agree + person; also means ally
Demonstrative; far from speaker, singular; distinct from tæ (conjunction)
Plural of ves via regular -ew rule
Plural of vek via regular -ew rule
